description: Length-balanced version of knot invariant facts. Target ratio: 0.8-1.3

facts.context: The Reidemeister R1 move (adding or removing a twist/kink) changes the writhe by:
facts.truth: plus or minus one, writhe changes under R1
distractors: zero, the writhe is always preserved by R1
plus or minus two per kink added
the writhe becomes undefined after R1

facts.context: The Reidemeister R2 and R3 moves change the writhe by:
facts.truth: zero, only R1 changes the writhe value
distractors: plus or minus one per R2 or R3 move
plus or minus two per move applied
writhe undefined after these moves

facts.context: The Kauffman bracket polynomial <K> under R1 (adding a positive kink) transforms as:
facts.truth: multiplied by minus A to the minus third power
distractors: the bracket polynomial K is unchanged by R1
multiplied by A to the first power only
the bracket polynomial changes sign only

facts.context: The Kauffman bracket polynomial <K> under R2 and R3 moves:
facts.truth: is invariant, preserved under R2 and R3
distractors: changes by a factor of A under these moves
is not defined after R2 or R3 applied
changes sign under R2 and R3 moves

facts.context: The Jones polynomial V(K) under all three Reidemeister moves R1, R2, R3:
facts.truth: is invariant, a true knot invariant
distractors: changes under R1 only, not R2 or R3
changes under all three R-moves applied
is only defined for alternating knots

facts.context: The Jones polynomial is related to the Kauffman bracket by:
facts.truth: V = bracket times writhe normalization factor
distractors: V equals the bracket directly, no change
V equals writhe times bracket, no more
they are completely unrelated quantities

facts.context: The Jones polynomial is invariant under R1 because:
facts.truth: writhe and bracket R1 changes cancel exactly
distractors: R1 does not affect the polynomial at all
the bracket is already invariant under R1
this invariance holds by definition only

facts.context: The Jones polynomial of the unknot (trivial knot) is:
facts.truth: equal to one, the simplest value possible
distractors: equal to zero, representing no crossings
equal to the variable t, linear in t
undefined for the unknot specifically

facts.context: The Jones polynomial of the right-handed trefoil knot is:
facts.truth: t plus t cubed minus t to the fourth
distractors: simply one, same as the unknot value
t squared only, a monomial result
t minus t squared, two term form

facts.context: The HOMFLY-PT polynomial P(a,z) is related to the Jones polynomial by:
facts.truth: Jones is a specialization of HOMFLY-PT
distractors: they are identical, the same polynomial
HOMFLY is simpler than Jones polynomial
HOMFLY is not a true knot invariant

facts.context: The Jones polynomial satisfies a skein relation that:
facts.truth: relates positive, negative, and zero crossings
distractors: says V of L+ equals V of L- exactly
does not exist for the Jones polynomial
says V(L+) plus V(L-) equals V(L0)

facts.context: The crossing number c(K) of a knot K is:
facts.truth: minimum crossings over all diagrams, invariant
distractors: crossings in any diagram, diagram dependent
not well-defined for composite knot types
changes under each Reidemeister move done

facts.truth: minimum crossing changes to make unknot
distractors: simply the number of crossings present
not an invariant, depends on diagram
always equals the crossing number c(K)

facts.context: For a two-component link L = L1 ∪ L2, the linking number lk(L1, L2) is:
facts.truth: half the sum of signed crossings between them
distractors: the total count of all crossings present
not an invariant, changes with diagrams
only defined for knots, not for links

facts.context: The figure-eight knot (4_1) has the special property of being:
facts.truth: amphichiral, equivalent to its mirror image
distractors: chiral, different from its mirror image
the same topologically as the trefoil
a composite knot, not a prime knot

facts.truth: trefoil from its mirror, and many from unknot
distractors: all possible knots from each other always
nothing useful about knot classification
only alternating knots from each other
